mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales capture broad consumer spending strength that directly lifts corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when RSAFS registers month-over-month growth above its historical median.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 25 basis points signals maximum monetary accommodation that suppresses short-term discount rates and pushes investors toward equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment and stagnant or falling payrolls indicate collapsing aggregate labor income and imminent demand contraction, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months where UNRATE increases month-over-month while PAYEMS decreases.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high joblessness, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal a manufacturing rebound from deep slack and excess capacity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO rises while UNRATE exceeds 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8% reflects severe underutilization of labor resources and weak household formation, which crushes consumer confidence and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in housing starts and real GDP reflect synchronized recovery in interest-sensitive spending and aggregate output, amplifying cyclical earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters when both HOUST and GDPC1 increase simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production provides a high-frequency signal of improving manufacturing utilization and operating leverage, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between INDPRO changes and 5-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero policy rates and elevated consumer price inflation produces deeply negative real interest rates that distort capital allocation and signal impending tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL exceeds its 12-month average.
